target.name: Wiliot
target.founder: Tal Tamir, Yaron Elboim, Alon Yehezkely

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $230M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Chips & Semiconductors. Range: $529M - $5.0B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Semiconductors P/S median is 24.2x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

private.name: DEEPX
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series C), post-money $529M

data.narrative:
Wiliot is a Series C semiconductor company that has raised $230M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a peer set of 4 same-sector and same/adjacent-stage private companies—SambaNova Systems ($5.0B Series D), Tenstorrent ($2.7B Series D), Positron AI ($1.0B Series B), and DEEPX ($529M Series C)—alongside reference to 3 public semiconductor comps (ALAB, NVDA, AMD).

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis, using a 1.7x step-up multiplier typical of Series C advancement, implies a $391M valuation. Peer-median analysis across the 4-company cohort yields $1.9B, with a range spanning $529M to $5.0B. Public-comp anchoring via price-to-sales (24.2x median across the 3 public names) was not applicable due to the absence of disclosed revenue figures for the target. The disparity between stage-progression and peer-median reflects uncertainty inherent in early-stage valuation and variation within the semiconductor subsector.

The triangulated analysis supports a range of $332M (low) to $2.1B (high), with a median of $1.1B. Key uncertainties include Wiliot's revenue run-rate relative to peers, product commercialization progress, and market traction in its specific application domain within semiconductors. The wide peer range ($529M to $5.0B) underscores the heterogeneity of funding outcomes even within this narrow cohort.
